title: The Book Job

jokes.text: These aren't dinosaurs! Dinosaurs sing! I want to go back in Mommy!
jokes.setup: Child at dinosaur exhibit expecting something specific
jokes.punchline: Child's illogical complaint that real dinosaurs don't sing like in movies
jokes.explanation: Good child logic absurdity, economical delivery

jokes.text: I paid $800 for five minutes!
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high price for brief experience
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent frustration with overpriced entertainment

jokes.text: How is that the lesson? The point of the dinosaurs is, no matter what we do, an asteroid's gonna wipe us out, so we should party hard and wreck the place!
jokes.setup: Educational message about taking care of Earth
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted nihilistic interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic subverting educational content into hedonistic philosophy

jokes.text: Yeah! Why should the asteroid have all the fun!
jokes.punchline: Bart personifying the asteroid as party-goer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation on Homer's premise with Bart's twisted enthusiasm

jokes.text: Lisa has a big toy. No fair. That means I get two small toys. No fair. This isn't big, it's medium! No fair! Then I get four small toys...
jokes.punchline: Escalating mathematical complexity and complaints
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of sibling negotiation complexity

jokes.text: Okay, Maggie gets a toy. No fair! No fair! Choke on fossil poop!
jokes.setup: Homer canceling all toys due to arguing
jokes.punchline: Exception for baby plus crude insult
jokes.explanation: Payoff to toy argument with mild shock humor

jokes.text: Dad, follow that dinosaur! I've waited my whole life to hear that.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Homer to follow the dinosaur
jokes.punchline: Homer's satisfaction at hearing this specific movie-like line
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor about action movie clichés with perfect Homer reaction

jokes.text: So tired. Go on without me.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic movie death scene parody from minor exertion
jokes.explanation: Good parody of dramatic sacrifice scenes applied to Homer's laziness

jokes.text: full of whimsy and, uh... chapters.
jokes.setup: Actress trying to sound like real fantasy author
jokes.punchline: Vague description ending with basic book component
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward pause humor showing character's unfamiliarity with books

jokes.text: Everybody knows you got the idea for this series after an explosion at a crumpet factory knocked you off a double-decker bus. How could that be made up?
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and British fake backstory
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd fake origin story that parodies overwrought author biographies

jokes.text: The plots are based on market research, and the pages are churned out by a room full of pill-popping lit majors desperate for work.
jokes.setup: Revealing truth about children's book industry
jokes.punchline: Cynical description of exploited writers
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of publishing industry with specific details that ring true

jokes.text: Sir, you're inside an Allosaurus. I demand to speak to my paleontologist!
jokes.punchline: Demanding paleontologist like it's customer service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic treating museum like business dispute

jokes.text: Doesn't it bother you that Betty Crocker is an invention of '20s-era ad men? I know you're hurting, but that's no reason to lash out at me.
jokes.setup: Lisa making comparison to corporate mascots
jokes.punchline: Marge taking it as personal attack
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Marge's defensive reaction to cultural critique

jokes.text: A million bucks?! I know five idiots!
jokes.setup: Learning about easy money in book publishing
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately thinking of exploiting his friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - immediately seeing opportunity and insulting his friends

jokes.text: Whatever the job is, I'm not interested. A million bucks has changed stupider minds than yours.
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal with insulting compliment
jokes.explanation: Good setup/reversal with Moe's characteristic insult-compliment

jokes.text: I like the beat, play me the tune. We're taking down kids who read. Chapter-book crowd? That's a juicy peach. But what's the cream?
jokes.setup: Moe trying to sound like professional criminal
jokes.punchline: Using food metaphors for target audience
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Moe's mix of criminal jargon and food terminology

jokes.text: Tween-lit gang-write? But this Babar needs a Zephyr. A Zephyr? You're the Zephyr.
jokes.punchline: Nonsense code words with children's book character
jokes.explanation: Moderately clever parody of heist movie jargon

jokes.text: Your friends are looking at my bloomers! Wash 'em again!
jokes.punchline: His mother's embarrassing interruption
jokes.explanation: Standard Skinner mother embarrassment, fits character well

jokes.text: With your share of the money, you could get your own studio apartment. The refrigerator could have my magnets on it.
jokes.setup: Trying to tempt Skinner with independence
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small dream of having his own fridge magnets
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Skinner's oppressed life with specific, sad detail

jokes.text: I'm fluent in every imaginary language, from Dothraki to Parseltongue.
jokes.setup: Patty explaining her fantasy novel credentials
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific nerd knowledge from unexpected character
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected character depth with current pop culture references

jokes.text: I wouldn't join one of your harebrained schemes for all the Japanese girlfriend pillows in Kyoto!
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate reference
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and specific reference that's memorably weird

jokes.text: I just adopted a capuchin monkey, and I can't leave it alone during the bonding phase.
jokes.setup: Lenny explaining why he can't join
jokes.punchline: Treating monkey adoption like serious commitment
jokes.explanation: Good Lenny weirdness with specific terminology

jokes.text: I did publish five modestly successful children's books.
jokes.setup: Carl trying to contribute to criminal enterprise
jokes.punchline: Unexpected literary credentials from background character
jokes.explanation: Nice character surprise but execution is understated

jokes.text: Like no one ever writes for money, Lisa. I don't see your boyfriend William Shakespeare missing too many meals.
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical take on Shakespeare's career
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic deflating Lisa's idealism

jokes.text: Wait, you're gonna be all the guys? How would that work?
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about singular writing
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat showing Homer's group-think mentality

jokes.text: I'll just bang out 2,000 words, and then I'll stop-- even if I'm on fire. I got to pace myself.
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic dedication to arbitrary word count
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of Lisa's intensity. 'Even if I'm on fire' is nice hyperbole for writing dedication.

jokes.text: Why is Bach next to Muddy Waters? That's my problem-- I got to get these CDs organized.
jokes.punchline: Procrastinating with organizational tasks
jokes.explanation: Relatable writer procrastination with Lisa's perfectionist character

jokes.text: Spot, stop, sop, top-tops, pot-pots, opt-opts... Post!
jokes.punchline: Getting increasingly excited about word game
jokes.explanation: Good procrastination sequence showing Lisa getting distracted

jokes.text: So many vampires! With the fangs and the capes and the medals-- nobody knows how they earned them.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific detail about mysterious vampire medals
jokes.explanation: Great specific observational detail about vampire imagery clichés

jokes.text: Everyone head for a different airport, and we meet in Rio in a year with new faces.
jokes.punchline: Moe treating book failure like major heist gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of heist movie parody to absurd conclusion

jokes.text: Hey, I ain't a troll. Look, I bleed red, just like you. Th-The first part is always green, but it turns red.
jokes.setup: Homer calling Moe a troll for story inspiration
jokes.punchline: Moe's defensive explanation including gross medical detail
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character moment with typical oversharing and defensiveness

jokes.text: The cheerleaders are pixies. The stoners are... gargoyles! And they play a complicated sport which makes no sense called Fuzzlepitch!
jokes.punchline: Absurd sport name that parodies Quidditch
jokes.explanation: Good escalating world-building with funny nonsense sport name

jokes.text: I don't care if he's the guy who wrote Sandman Volume One: Preludes and Nocturnes-- no one spies on us!
jokes.punchline: Specific comic book reference showing unexpected nerd knowledge
jokes.explanation: Great character surprise with Moe knowing specific Gaiman work

jokes.text: And lose the British accent. Cheeseburgers, French fries. I'm all over that, pal.
jokes.punchline: Immediately adopting American accent and slang
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo humor with accent change

jokes.text: God, I love being a writer!
jokes.setup: Lisa continuing to procrastinate in coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Declaring love for writing while doing everything but writing
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment about writer procrastination

jokes.text: Weekly Reader Star Selection good. I just hope we put in enough steampunk, whatever that is.
jokes.punchline: Homer's clueless reference to trendy genre
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Homer's ignorance of literary trends

jokes.text: Oh, you didn't write any of it. That tuna didn't salad itself.
jokes.setup: Carl claiming pride in their work
jokes.punchline: Homer pointing out Carl only made lunch
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Carl's contribution with specific detail

jokes.text: I'm a genius!
jokes.setup: Lisa coming up with mermaid love story
jokes.punchline: Declaring genius for obvious, overdone concept
jokes.explanation: Simple irony about Lisa thinking she's original with clichéd idea

jokes.text: Writing is the hardest thing ever!
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to complete frustration
jokes.explanation: Good emotional whiplash showing writer's emotional journey

jokes.text: I laminated the lunch menu and put them ia binder.
jokes.setup: Carl explaining his contribution to the book
jokes.punchline: Describing unnecessary organization of menus
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carl's helpful but pointless contribution

jokes.text: Where's your Franklin W. Dixon? Where's your T.R. Francis? Where's your Stephen King?
jokes.setup: Publisher explaining need for fake author
jokes.punchline: Including real author Stephen King in list of fake ones
jokes.explanation: Great joke implying Stephen King might be fake too

jokes.text: Let's acid melt him in a bathtub!
jokes.punchline: Moe's extreme solution for Gaiman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe violence escalation for minor problem

jokes.text: A dog wrote a bestseller?! All I've done is procrastinate!
jokes.setup: Lisa at book fair seeing competition
jokes.punchline: Devastating comparison to dog author
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with absurd comparison

jokes.text: I was raised in a traveling circus. My mother was a lady ringmaster, and my father was a lion barber. I wrote my first story with clown lipstick on a flattened popcorn box.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous circus details
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity with specific creative details like 'lion barber'

jokes.text: Is R.L. Stine here, 'cause you just gave me Goosebumps?
jokes.punchline: Pun on Goosebumps book series
jokes.explanation: Standard pun humor, gets job done but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Square as Golden Books, Pop.
jokes.setup: Moe asking if Kansas City debt is settled
jokes.punchline: Homer uses children's publisher name for 'square' idiom
jokes.explanation: Clever contextual twist on common phrase using book publisher

jokes.text: Not just by Milhouses. You'll get attention from Jacksons, Xanders, even Aidens. Aw, I've always wanted an Aiden.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's unexpected desire for an Aiden
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary on trendy names with Lisa's reaction

jokes.text: Before we got our hands on Twilight, it was about a girl who fell in love with a golem. But teenagers weren't going to spend their allowances to join Team Schmul.
jokes.punchline: Absurd original Twilight concept with Jewish folklore monster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody combining Twilight with Jewish mythology, 'Team Schmul' is perfect

jokes.text: teenagers weren't going to spend their allowances to join Team Schmul.
jokes.setup: Explaining why golem love story wouldn't work
jokes.punchline: Team Schmul vs Team Edward - Yiddish name sounds unmarketable
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of Twilight fan team names with ethnic comedy

jokes.text: If they had been in charge of the Sistine Chapel, the whole thing would be vampires, instead of the pope's private naked dude mural.
jokes.punchline: Describing Sistine Chapel as 'pope's private naked dude mural'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframing of famous art in crude, practical terms

jokes.text: Do the characters still say trolly instead of cool? - No. - Oh, that is so untrolly!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about troll slang they created
jokes.punchline: Using the made-up slang to express disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their created language with perfect usage

jokes.text: Hey, if you don't want your words changed, write a screenplay. We own your book, so why don't you go and cry into your million-dollar check?
jokes.punchline: Publisher's cynical dismissal with Hollywood reference
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire about creative control and money

jokes.text: I was gonna buy the apartment next door and fill it with dolls, but now that just sounds stupid.
jokes.punchline: Revealing creepy plan with sudden self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment revealing disturbing hobby plans

jokes.text: Now it's a dance contest at the vampire prom.
jokes.setup: Describing how publishers ruined their epic battle
jokes.punchline: Reduction to generic teen movie trope
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing how epic became clichéd

jokes.text: No, of course you didn't. The establishment I work for delivers pizzas to everyone, and then gives the customer the option of accepting or refusing delivery. That's a terrible business strategy. No, no, it's quite sound.
jokes.punchline: Defending obviously ridiculous business model
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic with Skinner maintaining character

jokes.text: Kansas City.
jokes.setup: Gaiman revealing he's the real villain
jokes.punchline: Single reference to their previous failed scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - two words explain everything

jokes.text: Kansas City.
jokes.punchline: Simple reference to their previous failed job
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect economy, maximum impact with minimal words

jokes.text: I bet it's the Earl of Marmalade over here.
jokes.setup: Looking for the traitor in their group
jokes.punchline: Moe's nickname for posh Neil Gaiman
jokes.explanation: Good character nickname showing Moe's view of British sophistication

jokes.text: I bet it's the Earl of Marmalade over here.
jokes.setup: Looking for traitor in their group
jokes.punchline: Mocking nickname for British Gaiman
jokes.explanation: 'Earl of Marmalade' is a good British stereotype insult. Moe's suspicion misdirection works well.

jokes.text: It's always a dame.
jokes.punchline: Film noir cliché delivered by Moe
jokes.explanation: Good genre reference with Moe's tough guy persona

jokes.text: My favorite theme of a wall calendar.
jokes.punchline: Describing password as oddly specific preference
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing absurd detail about password choice

jokes.text: Oh, by the way, the audio book is only available... abridged. Abridged!
jokes.punchline: Final insult of abridged audiobook plus horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Good final twist adding insult to injury

jokes.text: I got the idea from every movie ever made.
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's completely unoriginal
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about heist movie clichés

jokes.text: Poor Lisa. Did it never occur to her that there might be three flash drives? I've heisted my way to the bestseller list once again. And the most brilliant part is, I don't even know how to read.
jokes.punchline: Claiming illiteracy despite being famous author
jokes.explanation: Great final twist with absurd revelation about famous writer

jokes.text: Poor Lisa. Did it never occur to her that there might be three flash drives?
jokes.setup: Lisa thinking she outsmarted the publisher
jokes.punchline: Gaiman reveals he was playing everyone with even more complex scheme
jokes.explanation: Good triple-cross structure escalating the betrayals

jokes.text: It's simple, Moe. I don't like the taste... of poison. Ah... crap.
jokes.setup: Gaiman explaining why he didn't drink
jokes.punchline: Revealing he poisoned Moe's drink
jokes.explanation: Good dark twist with perfect dramatic timing and Moe's reaction

tmdb_overview: Homer creates a team, including author Neil Gaiman, to write the next hit "tween" book; after receiving an advance copy of the work from his publisher, Homer learns there's more to the business than he imagined.
